layout_description:
Option: OPT-1-FINAL
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN MARVIN TABLE LAYOUT with full-width title at top and full-width footer text at bottom. The slide uses a classic Marvin table structure (Section 4, Option A) to organize the two service offerings side by side as equal-weight columns. The layout follows a strict top-to-bottom vertical allocation: (1) Title area occupies the top 12% of slide height with a visible gap below it. (2) The two-column Marvin table body occupies the middle 70% of slide height, split equally between left and right columns with a small horizontal gutter. (3) Footer text occupies the bottom 10% of slide height with a visible top margin separating it from the table columns. (4) Remaining 8% distributed as margins and gaps. All elements snap to a 4-column horizontal grid with consistent left and right margins. The two content columns have IDENTICAL heights and IDENTICAL bottom edges (Rule B2 — mandatory horizontal alignment for Marvin tables). The footer container begins BELOW the bottom edge of both columns with a clear vertical gap — no overlap permitted. Content-aware spacing per Rule 0.1: bullet text font size increased to large (22-24pt range) and vertical spacing between bullet rows expanded to fill at least 70% of each column's inner height, eliminating bottom gaps. Dashed grey separator lines between bullet items create visual rhythm per Rule 0.10. The design applies brand colors by role name only, uses the Classic Marvin table pattern from Section 4 Option A, and ensures all text fits within containers without overflow or overlap.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 4
  Allocation: [0.12, 0.7, 0.1, 0.08]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Technology Platform): This container is the left column of a two-column Marvin table (Section 4, Option A). It occupies the left half of the slide body area, with equal width to the right column (split 1:1, equal-peers). CRITICAL COORDINATE CONSTRAINT from judge feedback: This container and C1 must have IDENTICAL heights and must end at EXACTLY the same Y-coordinate. The container must occupy 70% of the slide height allocation (per blueprint), positioned below the title area (which occupies 12%) with a visible top margin, and must end with sufficient bottom margin so that C2 (footer) can begin below it with a clear gap — no overlap permitted. The column structure from top to bottom: (1) Icon ornament — a small circular badge with a laptop/computer icon in brand accent color, positioned at the top-left of the column, sized proportionally to the header text height (small, jewel-like detail per Rule 0.4, approximately 24-28px diameter). (2) Column header — 'TECHNOLOGY PLATFORM' in bold, brand primary color, left-aligned, full column width. Header font size: heading font role, medium-large scale (18-20pt range). (3) Header underline — a solid horizontal line in brand dark color, full column width, thin stroke (1-2px), placed directly beneath the header text with no gap. (4) Subtitle — 'Cryptocurrency Analytics Capabilities' in italic body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a small top margin below the underline (8-12px). Subtitle font size: medium-large (18pt range) to maintain proportional hierarchy with the enlarged bullet text below. (5) Bullet list — six bullet items describing platform capabilities: 'Blockchain monitoring', 'Entity identification & attribution', 'Automated risk scoring', 'Analytics of VASP data', 'Investigation workbench', 'Dashboards & analytics'. Each bullet uses a small brand-color circle as the bullet marker (not native PowerPoint bullets), sized proportionally to the text height (6-8px diameter). CRITICAL REVISION per judge feedback: Bullet text font size increased to large (22-24pt range) to fill at least 70% of the container's inner vertical space, resolving the under-fill violation. Bullet text is body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset (12-16px from bullet marker). Bullets are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow on each bullet row section. CRITICAL SPACING REVISION: Vertical spacing (margin or padding) between consecutive bullet rows expanded significantly to distribute content across the full container height — the last bullet item must end near the bottom of the container with only minimal bottom padding remaining (no large gap at bottom). The spacing between bullet rows should be calculated as: (available_height_after_header_subtitle) / (number_of_bullet_rows) to ensure even distribution. (6) Dashed grey separator lines — thin horizontal dashed lines in light grey, full column width, placed between each consecutive bullet item (5 separators total, not after the last bullet). Separator stroke: thin (1px), dashed pattern (4px dash, 4px gap). With the increased font sizes and expanded spacing, the separators distribute naturally across the full container height, creating visual rhythm per Rule 0.10. The column has a small internal padding on all sides (16-20px). No background fill, no border on the column itself. Content must fill at least 70% of the container's inner vertical space, with bullet text and spacing occupying the full available height from subtitle bottom to container bottom.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Capacity Building): This container is the right column of a two-column Marvin table (Section 4, Option A). It occupies the right half of the slide body area, with equal width to the left column (split 1:1, equal-peers). CRITICAL COORDINATE CONSTRAINT from judge feedback: This container and C0 must have IDENTICAL heights and must end at EXACTLY the same Y-coordinate (Rule B2 — mandatory horizontal alignment for Marvin tables). The container must occupy 70% of the slide height allocation (per blueprint), positioned below the title area (which occupies 12%) with a visible top margin, and must end with sufficient bottom margin so that C2 (footer) can begin below it with a clear gap — no overlap permitted. The column structure from top to bottom: (1) Icon ornament — a small circular badge with a graduation cap icon in brand accent color, positioned at the top-left of the column, sized proportionally to the header text height, IDENTICAL size and style to the left column icon (small, jewel-like detail per Rule 0.4, approximately 24-28px diameter). (2) Column header — 'CAPACITY BUILDING' in bold, brand primary color, left-aligned, full column width. Header font size: heading font role, medium-large scale (18-20pt range), IDENTICAL to C0 header. (3) Header underline — a solid horizontal line in brand dark color, full column width, thin stroke (1-2px), placed directly beneath the header text with no gap. (4) Subtitle — 'Training & Knowledge Transfer' in italic body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a small top margin below the underline (8-12px). Subtitle font size: medium-large (18pt range), IDENTICAL to C0 subtitle, to maintain proportional hierarchy with the enlarged bullet text below and preserve within-slide uniformity (Rule C6). (5) Bullet list — six bullet items describing training offerings: 'Foundation training for all staff', 'Practitioner certification', 'Specialist programs', 'Leadership briefings', 'Hands-on labs', 'Case study workshops'. Each bullet uses a small brand-color circle as the bullet marker (not native PowerPoint bullets), sized proportionally to the text height (6-8px diameter), IDENTICAL to C0 bullet markers. CRITICAL REVISION per judge feedback: Bullet text font size increased to large (22-24pt range), IDENTICAL to C0 bullet text, to fill at least 70% of the container's inner vertical space and resolve the under-fill violation. Bullet text is body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset (12-16px from bullet marker). Bullets are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow on each bullet row section, with flexGrow values IDENTICAL to C0 so that row boundaries align horizontally across both columns (Rule B2 — horizontal alignment of row sections is mandatory for Marvin tables). CRITICAL SPACING REVISION: Vertical spacing (margin or padding) between consecutive bullet rows expanded significantly, IDENTICAL to C0 spacing calculations, to distribute content across the full container height — the last bullet item must end near the bottom of the container with only minimal bottom padding remaining (no large gap at bottom). The spacing between bullet rows should be calculated as: (available_height_after_header_subtitle) / (number_of_bullet_rows) to ensure even distribution, using the SAME calculation as C0. (6) Dashed grey separator lines — thin horizontal dashed lines in light grey, full column width, placed between each consecutive bullet item (5 separators total, not after the last bullet). Separator stroke: thin (1px), dashed pattern (4px dash, 4px gap). The separators must be positioned at EXACTLY the same vertical Y-coordinates as the separators in C0 to enforce horizontal alignment (Rule B2). With the increased font sizes and expanded spacing applied identically to both columns, the separators distribute naturally across the full container height in both columns, maintaining perfect horizontal alignment and creating visual rhythm per Rule 0.10. The column has a small internal padding on all sides (16-20px), IDENTICAL to C0. No background fill, no border on the column itself. Content must fill at least 70% of the container's inner vertical space, with bullet text and spacing occupying the full available height from subtitle bottom to container bottom, matching C0 fill ratio exactly.
  C2 (FOOTER TEXT — Partnership Statement): This container holds the footer text that sits at the bottom of the slide, below the two-column Marvin table. CRITICAL COORDINATE CONSTRAINT from judge feedback: This container must be positioned BELOW the bottom edge of both C0 and C1 with a VISIBLE TOP MARGIN (at least 20-30 pixels vertical gap) to prevent overlap. The previous attempt positioned C2 too high, causing text-on-text overlap with the last bullet items in both columns. This must be corrected by the CSS layout agent: C2 top edge Y-coordinate must be greater than (C0 bottom edge Y-coordinate + 20-30px gap). The container spans the full slide width with the same left and right margins as the title area. The container occupies 10% of the slide height allocation (per blueprint), positioned in the bottom region of the slide. The text reads: 'Log-term partnerships including support training and ability to adapt and provide solutions to emerging needs' (note: the source text contains 'Log-term' which appears to be a typo for 'Long-term', but is preserved as-is per content fidelity rules). The text is rendered in body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a visible top margin separating it from the bottom edge of the table columns above (this top margin is part of the gap requirement, not internal container padding). Font size is standard body size (16-18pt range). No background fill, no border, no decorative elements. The text wraps naturally to 2-3 lines depending on final font size and slide width, with a small bottom margin from the slide edge (8-12px). This is a plain text block, not a colored footer bar. The container height is compact — just enough to contain the text with minimal internal padding above and below (8-12px top/bottom padding). The container must fit entirely within the slide bounds without being clipped.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER — Inactive): The bottom decorative layer is NOT ACTIVE for this slide. The two-column Marvin table structure fills the slide body area effectively, and with the revised font sizes (22-24pt bullet text) and expanded vertical spacing, the content density now fills at least 70% of each column's inner height. The columns are visually unified by their shared structure (matching headers, underlines, bullet styles, and horizontally aligned separator lines) without requiring a background card or panel. Per Rule D1, the decorative layer is used only when content would leave the slide looking empty or when visual grouping cannot be achieved through container design alone — neither condition applies here. The revised layout resolves the under-fill issue through increased font sizes and expanded spacing within the containers, not through addition of a decorative background element. The slide background remains white or the template default background color.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER — Inactive): The top connector layer is NOT ACTIVE for this slide. There are no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements required. The two columns are independent content sections with no visual links between them. Per Rule E3, the top layer is used only for elements that genuinely span containers or sit above all containers — no such elements exist in this layout. All visual elements are contained within their respective containers (C0, C1, C2).
